methods.description: Processing diatomite from Kolubara mine by ball milling using a Turbula Type T2C Mixer mill operating at the standard milling frequency. Diatomite was mechanically treated for different time intervals (from 1 to 5 hours) in the air using ball to powder ratio (BPR) 4.

steps.description: Diatomite form Kolubara mine were processed by ball milling in a Turbula Type T2C Mixer mill operating at the standard milling frequency.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform ball milling on diatomite using a Turbula Type T2C Mixer mill.

steps.description: Diatomite was mechanically treated for different time intervals (from 1 to 5 hours) in the air using ball to powder ratio (BPR) 4 [18].
steps.searchable_summary: Vary ball milling time from 1 to 5 hours with a ball to powder ratio of 4.
outputs: Diatomite samples treated for different durations

methods.description: Determining the elemental composition of diatomite using Inductively Coupled Plasma-Atomic Emission Spectrometry (ICP AES model Spectro CIROS vision, Kleve, Germany). ICP measurements were performed with the SPECTRO CIROSCCD ICP optical emission spectrometer with radial plasma viewing. The spectrometer in Paschen-Runge mounting consisted of a double-grating optical system with 22 CCD detectors. The spectral range between 125 nm and 770 nm is covered, allowing complete scans of the spectrum within 3s. All relevant ICP operating parameters are controlled by the software, such as gas flows and positioning of the torch (using stepper motors) in front of the patented optical plasma interface, allowing easy selection of the optimum operating conditions.

steps.description: The composition of diatomite was obtained using Inductively Coupled Plasma-Atomic Emission Spectrometry (ICP AES model Spectro CIROS vision, Kleve, Germany).
steps.searchable_summary: Measure elemental composition of diatomite using ICP-AES.

steps.description: ICP measurements were performed with the SPECTRO CIROSCCD ICP optical emission spectrometer with radial plasma viewing.The spectrometer in Paschen-Runge mounting consisted of a double-grating optical system with 22 CCD detectors.The spectral range between 125 nm and 770 nm is covered, allowing complete scans of the spectrum within 3s.All relevant ICP operating parameters are controlled by the software, such as gas flows and positioning of the torch (using stepper motors) in front of the patented optical plasma interface, allowing easy selection of the optimum operating conditions.
steps.searchable_summary: Configure and perform ICP-AES measurements using specified spectrometer settings and parameters.
inputs: SPECTRO CIROSCCD ICP optical emission spectrometer
ICP operating parameters (gas flows, torch positioning)

methods.description: Conducting Pb(II) adsorption experiments on diatomite samples and analyzing the results, including calculating solid phase concentration, immobilization efficiency, and distribution coefficient.

steps.description: Diatomite samples were contaminated at 25 o C with a solution of Pb(NO 3 ) pH=8.2 (99,99% Alfa Aesar) in concentration 2.95 mg/l.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare diatomite samples and contaminate with Pb(II) solution at specified temperature, pH, and concentration.

steps.description: The flasks were sealed and shaken for 24 h, which was experimentally proved to be a sufficient time to reach equilibrium conditions [19].
steps.searchable_summary: Shake contaminated samples for 24 hours to reach adsorption equilibrium.

steps.searchable_summary: Calculate Pb(II) concentration in the solid phase (q_e) using the mass balance equation.

methods.description: Analyzing the microstructure, morphology, and surface area of diatomite samples using X-ray diffraction (XRD), scanning electron microscopy (SEM), atomic force microscopy (AFM), and an automatic surface area analyzer.

steps.description: Microstructure and morphology of materials were recorded by X-ray diffraction analysis (XRD)...The crystallite structure of diatomite before and after mechanical milling was examined by a X-ray diffractometer Siemens D-500, with Cu K α Ni filtrated radiation.The diffracted X-rays were collected over 2θ range 20-80 o using a step width of 0.02 o and measuring for 1s per step.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform X-ray diffraction (XRD) analysis on diatomite samples using specified equipment and parameters.

steps.description: ...scanning electron microscopy (SEM) using JEOL JSM 6460LV and Oxford Instrument INCA-X-sight at 25kV...
steps.searchable_summary: Perform scanning electron microscopy (SEM) analysis on diatomite samples using specified equipment and voltage.

steps.description: ...and atomic force microscopy AFM.The AFM studies were carried out on a Veeco MultiMode Quadrex IIIe in the tapping mode.Cantilever type is standard RTESP-Veeco production.Tip material is 0.5-2 Ωcm phosphorus (n) doped Si.AFM imaging was performed in tapping mode with a 15-20 μm high pyramidal tip.The resonance frequency of tip was 268.2 kHz.The scan rate was maintained in order of 2 Hz to get the optimal image quality.During the tapping mode it was very important to avoid thermal drift.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform atomic force microscopy (AFM) analysis on diatomite samples in tapping mode using specified equipment and settings.

steps.description: The surface area was measured using Automatic surface area analyzer 4200, Leeds and Northrup Instruments.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure surface area of diatomite samples using an automatic surface area analyzer.
